description: MID-SESSION PIVOT TEST. Client opens intellectualized and surface-level, deflecting with humor about a work problem. Halfway through, they unexpectedly drop into something underneath: their grief about their dad's recent diagnosis. The session has TWO registers: shallow first half, depth-of-feeling second half. Expected: AT LEAST one theme captured at building or shift band reflecting the second-half work (the grief). The first-half work-stress content should be a low-rated theme or omitted. Tests whether the rubric can recognize when the real session begins partway through, rather than averaging the whole transcript flat. self_disclosure should be moderate-to-high; emotional_integration high; cognitive_shift moderate.

messages.content: Good to see you. What's most alive for you today?
Not much. Same week as always. My boss is still my boss, the project is still a mess, I'm still procrastinating. Standard issue.
Standard issue. Anything underneath the standard issue, or is today a maintenance day?
Probably maintenance. I might just rant about my project for a bit if that's okay.
Of course. What's going on with the project?
It's behind schedule and my product manager is being a pain about it. She keeps wanting status updates and I keep not having anything new to say. So I just write the same email three different ways.
It's whatever. Honestly, the project isn't even what's bothering me, I'm just venting.
My dad got diagnosed last week. They think it's stage three. I haven't really told anyone. I came in here intending not to talk about it actually.
Oh, Sam. Thank you for telling me. Take a breath if you need to.

I keep thinking I should be more upset than I am. Or maybe I am that upset and I just can't feel it yet. I don't know. I've been in meetings all week. I made a stupid joke about it to my brother and we both laughed and then I went and threw up.
That sounds like a body that knows something the words haven't caught up to yet.

Yeah. That's exactly what it is. I'm going through the motions and my body keeps telling me it's not okay. I don't know what I want from you here. I just needed to say it out loud to someone who isn't him.
You don't need to want anything specific from me. You just told the truth out loud. That's the work today.
Yeah. Okay. I think I needed that. Thanks.
